event_comments: Thank you to all volunteers for helping make this event a success!
cumulative_event_comments:
The sixth and final event in the 2019 Wednesday Evening Series will be held at North Seattle College! The Wednesday Evening Series is our most relaxed, casual set of navigation events, held in local parks once the weather warms up. These events are a little more low-key than those in the Winter League, and we typically offer a barbeque afterward for all participants. Decompress after work and bring a coworker or two along. Or meet your family at the park and enjoy a night off from cooking. Or meet a bunch of friends at the park for an evening of exploring together, chatting over some food, and finishing up with frisbee on the lawn.
Events in the Wednesday Evening Series are generally less challenging (both physically and navigationally) than many of our other events, and are therefore great for beginners and those less experienced with orienteering – while also offering a challenge to longtime participants of the sport.
North Seattle College is an urban campus surrounded by park land. The college buildings here are unusually complex with several levels so you may need to go up or down to reach your controls. The map has some special symbols showing the outlines of buildings and passageways on the ground floor, which would otherwise be hidden under the floors above, so check out the legend on the map (http://cascadeoc.org/map/north-seattle-college/) or ask someone at the event. Note that the map is in accordance with the ISSOM 2007 sprint map standard, which includes some uncrossable symbols (not allowed to cross). Out-of-bounds areas (not allowed to enter) are marked on the map with magenta stripes or olive-green color, and a flower on the control description list.
One SAFETY note: Course 3 and 4 will cross over the busy College Way to Licton Springs Park and there are two points of legal crossing. They are well marked on the map. Please cross only at these two crosswalks and be careful.
